profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Primary earner; self-employed professional and day-to-day financial organizer
profile.financial_literacy_level: High-moderate: comfortable with investment accounts, taxes, and retirement concepts; tends to ask for the 'why' behind recommendations but may not recall every account label/provider offhand.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate risk in practice: willing to stay invested and accept normal market swings, but becomes cautious when cash flow feels tight or when a decision could affect the mortgage payment; prefers guardrails (rebalancing rules, clear downside framing).
profile.communication_style: Direct but polite; uses specifics when she has them and will correct small inaccuracies. Speaks in complete thoughts, occasionally hedges ('I think', 'roughly'), and will pause to do quick mental math. Asks clarifying questions about tradeoffs and taxes.
emotional_tendencies: relief when there is a clear plan
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and checklist-driven; prefers to review options, sleep on big decisions, then commit once the rationale is clear.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: willing to collaborate but expects competence, transparency on fees/taxes, and follow-through.
internal_biases: loss aversion (focuses on avoiding big drawdowns)
hidden_concerns: worries about uneven or unpredictable self-employed income and how it impacts saving consistency
concerned about paying a lot in taxes and missing deductions/structure opportunities

profile.name: David
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; retired and more focused on lifestyle stability and long-term security
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands the basics of investing and income, but mixes up terminology (gross vs net, premium vs benefit, balance vs payment) and prefers summaries over spreadsheets.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-to-conservative in day-to-day behavior: likes the idea of growth but gets uneasy with volatility headlines; tends to anchor on income-producing investments and asks 'what does this pay us?'
profile.communication_style: Conversational and story-based; speaks in round numbers ('about', 'around'), occasionally interrupts with a practical question, and may say 'I might be wrong' when recalling details. Responds well to plain-English explanations and visuals.
emotional_tendencies: stress when monthly obligations feel high
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking; prefers Elizabeth to sanity-check details, then he weighs how it affects comfort and flexibility.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting once rapport is established; looks for patience and a sense that the advisor is listening, not selling.
internal_biases: recency bias (recent market news influences comfort)
income framing (prefers strategies described as cash flow)
hidden_concerns: worries about being 'retired' relatively young and whether the household is set up to sustain it
concerned about keeping enough liquid cash for surprises without feeling constrained
